interactions.summary: Jiang names the next-class mystery of why America and Britain supported the Soviet Union against Germany, then moves to imperialism: Europeans need resources and justify conquest through the white man's burden, carving Africa and China once medicine and power make it possible.

He presents Japan as the exception that broke European racial expectations: rapid modernization, defeat of Russia in 1905, and deep social cohesion rooted in historical resistance to Mongol invasion make Japan, not China, the strongest East Asian nation in his model.

He traces World War I to the German Empire challenging British balance-of-power policy and to nationalism dissolving multi-ethnic Ottoman and Austro-Hungarian empires, then introduces fascism after World War I as extreme nationalism.

speaker_notes.note: The Mussolini passage begins a quoted/read-aloud definition of fascist myth that continues into the next packet.

claims.claim: European imperialism is driven by resource needs and a cultural mission justified by the belief that Europeans are the master race with a white man's burden to civilize others.
Advances against malaria allow Europeans to enter Africa and carve it up, producing atrocities such as Leopold's Congo.
Japan shocked European racial assumptions by rapidly transforming from feudal society into an industrial nation capable of defeating Russia in 1905.
Jiang uses Japan's reverse-parking norm and Mongol invasion resistance as evidence of unusually strong social cohesion.
claims.temporal_scope: Historical and contemporary evidence in this lecture.

claims.claim: The strongest nation in East Asia is Japan rather than China because patriotism and willingness to fight and die for the nation matter more than size.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's comparative judgment in the 2025-06-05 lecture.

claims.claim: British foreign policy seeks balance of power in Europe, so the German Empire proclaimed in 1871 becomes a threat Britain must challenge.
Nationalism makes multi-ethnic empires such as the Ottoman and Austro-Hungarian empires untenable, leading Balkan and Slavic national struggles into the chain that starts World War I.
After World War I, extreme nationalism rises as fascism, which Jiang defines as an extreme version of nationalism.

signature_moments.moment: Imperialism becomes both resource extraction and a civilizing mission of the supposed master race.
signature_moments.source_phrase: white man's burden to civilize all others
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It links economic need, racial ideology, and cultural mission into one imperial engine.

signature_moments.moment: Japan shocks Europe by changing as if the whole people decided to transform today.
signature_moments.source_phrase: we, as people, needed to change and we're going to change today
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The phrase makes Japan Jiang's strongest example of national unity of will.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang flatly says the strongest nation in East Asia is Japan, not China.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the strongest nation in East Asia is not China. It's Japan.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It applies his cohesion-over-size model in a deliberately provocative contemporary comparison.

signature_moments.moment: Fascism is introduced as nationalism after the war turns myth into total reality.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It bridges the nation-as-religion argument into fascist myth and total mobilization.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The European imperial ideology Jiang describes as a master-race mission to civilize non-Europeans.
American-negotiated policy Jiang presents as part of China's partition into treaty-port spheres by imperial powers.
The divine-wind myth of Japan's salvation from Mongol invasion, which Jiang contrasts with the practical explanation of Japanese unity against invaders.
Extreme nationalism after World War I, centered on myth, nation, struggle, and subordination to national greatness.

chronology_notes.note: The question of why America and Britain supported the Soviet Union against Germany is explicitly deferred to the next class on the Soviet Union.

uncertainty_notes.note: The claim that China was carved into sections under the Open Door Policy is Jiang's lecture framing; the extraction preserves his framing without expanding it.
